sections.summary: Subscribers must be informed about directory purposes and usage before inclusion in public directories.
sections.dtia_summary: Subscribers must be informed about directory purposes and usage before inclusion in public directories.

requirements.text: Member States shall ensure that subscribers are informed, free of charge and before they are included in public directories, about the purposes and usage of the directory.

sections.heading: The service provider must inform the subscriber or user
sections.summary: Service providers must inform subscribers of traffic data types and processing duration for billing and marketing purposes.
sections.dtia_summary: Service providers must inform subscribers of traffic data types and processing duration for billing and marketing purposes.

requirements.text: The service provider must inform the subscriber or user of the types of traffic data which are processed and of the duration of such processing for the purposes mentioned in paragraph 2 and, prior to obtaining consent, for the purposes mentioned in paragraph 3.

sections.source_quote: The service provider must inform the subscriber or user of the types of traffic data which are processed and of the duration of such processing for the purposes mentioned in paragraph 2 and, prior to obtaining consent, for the purposes mentioned in paragraph 3.

sections.heading: Processing of traffic data, in accordance with paragraphs
sections.summary: Traffic data processing must be restricted to persons authorized for billing, traffic management, fraud detection, marketing, or value-added services.
sections.dtia_summary: Traffic data processing must be restricted to authorized personnel for billing, traffic management, fraud detection, marketing, or value-added services.

requirements.text: Processing of traffic data, in accordance with paragraphs 1, 2, 3 and 4, must be restricted to persons acting under the authority of providers of the public communications networks and publicly available electronic communications services handling billing or traffic management, customer enquiries, fraud detection, marketing electronic communications services or providing a value added service, and must be restricted to what is necessary for the purposes of such activities.

sections.source_quote: Processing of traffic data, in accordance with paragraphs 1, 2, 3 and 4, must be restricted to persons acting under the authority of providers of the public communications networks and publicly available electronic communications services handling billing or traffic management, customer enquiries, fraud detection, marketing electronic communications services or providing a value added service, and must be restricted to what is necessary for the purposes of such activities.
